Evening skateboard ride home; distance is manageable and fits Marcus's transportation style. Light traffic on a Wednesday night.

beats.activity: Listen to vinyl records, browse design inspiration, wind down
beats.reasoning: Night owl Marcus unwinds at home with music and creative browsing; this positions him at home before the final sleep beat and reflects his love of vinyl records.
Marcus sleeps 7.25 hours, ending at 6:30am the next morning. Night owl schedule means later bedtime but still gets restorative rest.

diary: Woke up to that perfect early June light and just felt like moving today. Skateboarded down to Ballard Starbucks with my coffee order already mapped out in my head—got some logo work done before the World Cup match. Caught USA vs Wales at Ballard Brew Shop with lunch, the energy there was electric even though it wasn't packed. After that, Capitol Hill called me. Hit up Sonic Boom to dig through their new vinyl—grabbed a couple records that caught my ear and got lost talking music with the staff. Set up shop at Espresso Vivace for the afternoon, knocked out some website mockups and brand guidelines. Solo dinner at Matt's in the Market felt right; just watched the neighborhood move around me while I ate. Got home, spun those new records, scrolled through design stuff online. There's something about days like this—nothing extraordinary, but everything lined up just right. Felt present the whole time.
thought_process: The light rail closure didn't really affect my route since I was already planning to skateboard everywhere—honestly kind of preferred it. The World Cup match was perfect timing; I needed a break around mid-morning and being around other people cheering felt good. I chose Capitol Hill because I was restless after the match, needed to move between neighborhoods rather than hunker down at one spot. The weather made skateboarding feel less like transit and more like part of the day itself. By afternoon, I was in a good headspace for focused work, so I stayed put at Vivace. Solo dinner felt intentional, not lonely—I was just absorbing the city around me. The records at home felt like the right ending note.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District for maintenance until 3pm.
